rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical hydrodifluoromethylation of alkenes (and double hydrodifluoromethylation of alkynes) using PhSO2CF2H as a CF2H radical source with a sacrificial Zn anode and carbon cathode in MeCN/piperidine with H2O as proton source.
electrochemical_role: Cathodic single-electron reduction of the (hetero)aryl sulfone PhSO2CF2H (E_red = -2.3 V vs Fc/Fc+) generates a CF2H radical with extrusion of ArSO2-; the Zn anode is sacrificially oxidized (E_ox = +0.01 V). A second cathodic SET reduces the benzylic radical adduct (E_red = -2.1 V) to a carbanion, which is protonated by H2O (or piperidinium).

key_intermediates.name: CF2H radical (from sulfone fragmentation after SET)
benzylic C-radical from CF2H addition to alkene
benzylic carbanion (after second SET at cathode)
vinyl radical (in alkyne variant, after first CF2H addition)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces PhSO2CF2H by SET to release sulfinate and a CF2H radical, which adds to the alkene to give a benzylic radical; a second cathodic SET reduces this radical to a benzylic carbanion that is protonated by H2O (or piperidine/PhSH HAT in the alkyne case). The Zn anode is oxidized to Zn2+ to balance charge.

probe_reagents.name: D2O (10 equiv) instead of H2O
probe_reagents.observation: 58% D incorporation at the benzylic (proton-quench) position of product 3-d, consistent with carbanion protonation by water.

probe_reagents.name: Radical clock 6a (1-phenyl-1-vinylcyclopropane, R1=Ph)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 8a obtained in 36%; closed product 7a not observed (k_Ph ~ 10^8 s^-1), indicating a C-radical intermediate fast enough to ring-open.

probe_reagents.name: Radical clock 6b (vinylcyclopropane, R1=H)
probe_reagents.observation: Closed cyclopropane product 7b in 99%; ring-opened 8b not observed (k_H ~ 10^5 s^-1), consistent with radical addition but slower clock not outrunning trapping.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry / voltammetric profile
probe_reagents.observation: Anode plateaus at ~ +0.01 V (Zn0 -> Zn2+); cathode operates near -2.1 to -2.3 V, matching reduction of PhSO2CF2H (-2.3 V) and of the benzylic radical (-2.1 V).

notes: For the alkyne double hydrodifluoromethylation (Supplementary Figure 7), PhSH is invoked as a HAT mediator to convert the initial vinyl radical to an alkene that undergoes a second CF2H radical addition before cathodic reduction/protonation; this HAT step is specific to the alkyne variant and not required in the alkene case. The 58% (not 100%) D incorporation suggests competing proton sources (piperidine/piperidinium) in addition to D2O.
